summary: The empty-needs audit is useful diagnostic input, but it does not authorize dependency mutation. Exact exam or target-exercise evidence is required before applying prerequisite edges.
accepted_outcomes: R2.1/R2.2 audit and review packet are accepted as diagnostic inputs.
Foundational A-domain units may be treated as underbouw_assumed rather than given artificial platform prerequisites.
The elasticity chain direction is pedagogically useful but still needs evidence anchors before mutation.
blocked_outcomes: Do not apply empty-needs dependency corrections through CLI yet.
Do not treat machine suggestions as accepted human decisions.
Do not proceed to authoritative prerequisite graph work from this packet.
Do not proceed to R3.2 as the immediate next sprint.

explicit_decisions.decision: Hold all dependency corrections until exact exam or target-exercise evidence is attached per edge.
Treat foundational A-domain empty-needs units as underbouw_assumed where appropriate.
A15 Prijselasticiteit van de vraag should depend on A38 Percentage change, and A16/A17 should route through A15, but mutation waits for exact evidence.
Treat D04 as a unit-design issue, likely merge/retire/redistribute into the relevant elasticity units.
Reject D10 -> D01/D03, D13 -> D03, and D32 -> D01/D03 as wrong-layer or too advanced.
Hold labor-market and unemployment sequencing for a separate second pedagogical pass.

conditions_to_reopen_or_pass: Attach exact exam or target-exercise evidence to candidate dependency edges.
Resolve D04 unit scope before applying any D04 dependency edge.
Classify foundational A-domain empty-needs units with an explicit workflow.
Run a separate labor-market/unemployment sequencing review.
Keep all machine-reference mutations behind CLI scripts.
